answer:
\boxed{J}

The observed shift of the hydrogen spectral line from **486.1 nm** to **485.9 nm** indicates a **blueshift**, meaning the star is moving **toward us**. This is due to the **Doppler effect**, where light from an approaching source appears slightly blueshifted. None of the other options (brightness, size, temperature, rotation, etc.) explain this wavelength change.
